1	# AGENTS.md
2	
3	## Task Completion Requirements
4	
5	- Both `bun lint` and `bun typecheck` must pass before considering tasks completed.
6	- NEVER run `bun test`. Always use `bun run test` (runs Vitest).
7	
8	## Project Snapshot
9	
10	T3 Code is a minimal web GUI for using code agents like Codex and Claude Code (coming soon).
11	
12	This repository is a VERY EARLY WIP. Proposing sweeping changes that improve long-term maintainability is encouraged.
13	
14	## Core Priorities
15	
16	1. Performance first.
17	2. Reliability first.
18	3. Keep behavior predictable under load and during failures (session restarts, reconnects, partial streams).
19	
20	If a tradeoff is required, choose correctness and robustness over short-term convenience.
21	
22	## Maintainability
23	
24	Long term maintainability is a core priority. If you add new functionality, first check if there are shared logic that can be extracted to a separate module. Duplicate logic across mulitple files is a code smell and should be avoided. Don't be afraid to change existing code. Don't take shortcuts by just adding local logic to solve a problem.
25	
26	## Package Roles
27	
28	- `apps/server`: Node.js WebSocket server. Wraps Codex app-server (JSON-RPC over stdio), serves the React web app, and manages provider sessions.
29	- `apps/web`: React/Vite UI. Owns session UX, conversation/event rendering, and client-side state. Connects to the server via WebSocket.
30	- `packages/contracts`: Shared effect/Schema schemas and TypeScript contracts for provider events, WebSocket protocol, and model/session types. Keep this package schema-only — no runtime logic.
31	- `packages/shared`: Shared runtime utilities consumed by both server and web. Uses explicit subpath exports (e.g. `@t3tools/shared/git`) — no barrel index.
32	
33	## Codex App Server (Important)
34	
35	T3 Code is currently Codex-first. The server starts `codex app-server` (JSON-RPC over stdio) per provider session, then streams structured events to the browser through WebSocket push messages.
36	
37	How we use it in this codebase:
38	
39	- Session startup/resume and turn lifecycle are brokered in `apps/server/src/codexAppServerManager.ts`.
40	- Provider dispatch and thread event logging are coordinated in `apps/server/src/providerManager.ts`.
41	- WebSocket server routes NativeApi methods in `apps/server/src/wsServer.ts`.
42	- Web app consumes orchestration domain events via WebSocket push on channel `orchestration.domainEvent` (provider runtime activity is projected into orchestration events server-side).
43	
44	Docs:
45	
46	- Codex App Server docs: https://developers.openai.com/codex/sdk/#app-server
47	
48	## Reference Repos
49	
50	- Open-source Codex repo: https://github.com/openai/codex
51	- Codex-Monitor (Tauri, feature-complete, strong reference implementation): https://github.com/Dimillian/CodexMonitor
52	
53	Use these as implementation references when designing protocol handling, UX flows, and operational safeguards.
54

Excellent! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me create a detailed summary document:

## Comprehensive T3 Code Project Exploration Report

Based on my thorough analysis of the T3 Code project, here's what I found:

---

## 1. Overall Directory Structure

```
.
├── apps/
│   ├── web/                 # React/Vite web app (our focus)
│   ├── server/              # Node.js WebSocket server
│   ├── desktop/             # Electron/Tauri desktop app
│   └── marketing/           # Marketing website
├── packages/
│   ├── shared/              # Shared runtime utilities (git, model, logging, shell, Net)
│   └── contracts/           # Shared Effect/Schema schemas and TypeScript contracts
├── scripts/                 # Build and dev scripts
└── [config files]          # Root-level configs (tsconfig, turbo, package.json)
```

---

## 2. Web App Structure (apps/web)

### Root Files:
- **package.json** - React 19, Vite 8 beta, TanStack Router/Query, Zustand, Tailwind v4
- **tsconfig.json** - Extends base tsconfig; jsx: "react-jsx"; path alias `~/*` → `./src/*`
- **vite.config.ts** - Uses Tailwind CSS Vite plugin, TanStack Router plugin, React Compiler
- **components.json** - Shadcn config; style: "base-mira"; baseColor: zinc; CSS variables enabled

### Key src/ Directories:

```
src/
├── main.tsx                           # Entry point - React 19 with RouterProvider
├── index.css                          # Main CSS file (see section 3 below)
├── router.ts                          # Router setup with QueryClient & StoreProvider
├── store.ts                           # Zustand store + StoreProvider (see section 4)
├── hooks/
│   ├── useTheme.ts                    # Light/dark/system theme management
│   ├── useMediaQuery.ts               # Media query hook
│   └── useTurnDiffSummaries.ts        # Domain-specific hook
├── components/
│   ├── ui/                            # Base-UI + Shadcn components (40+ UI components)
│   ├── DiffWorkerPoolProvider.tsx     # Theme-aware diff worker provider
│   ├── ChatView.tsx                   # Main chat view component
│   ├── Sidebar.tsx                    # Sidebar with projects/threads
│   └── [other major components]
├── lib/
│   ├── utils.ts                       # cn() function (tailwind-merge + CVA)
│   ├── diffRendering.ts               # Theme name resolution (pierre-light, pierre-dark)
│   └── [react-query, server, provider configs]
├── routes/
│   ├── __root.tsx                     # Root route with event handling
│   ├── _chat.$threadId.tsx            # Chat view route
│   ├── _chat.settings.tsx             # Settings route
│   └── _chat.tsx                      # Chat layout
├── appSettings.ts                     # App-level settings (model, service tier, etc.)
├── types.ts                           # TypeScript type definitions
├── nativeApi.ts                       # WebSocket API bridge
└── [various domain stores/logic]
```

---

## 3. CSS & Theming System

### index.css (Complete Theming Strategy)

**Framework**: Tailwind CSS v4 with custom properties and @theme directive

**Key Features**:

1. **CSS Custom Properties (variables)** defined in `:root`:
   ```css
   --background, --foreground
   --card, --card-foreground
   --primary, --primary-foreground
   --secondary, --secondary-foreground
   --muted, --muted-foreground
   --accent, --accent-foreground
   --destructive, --destructive-foreground
   --ring, --border, --input
   --info, --success, --warning (and their foreground variants)
   --radius (base), --radius-sm, --radius-md, --radius-lg, --radius-xl, --radius-2xl, --radius-3xl, --radius-4xl
   ```

2. **Light Theme** (default):
   - Background: white
   - Foreground: neutral-800
   - Primary: oklch(0.488 0.217 264) - purple-ish
   - Secondary/Muted/Accent: alpha(black / 4%)

3. **Dark Theme** (via `.dark` selector):
   - Background: color-mix(neutral-950 95%, white)
   - Foreground: neutral-100
   - Primary: oklch(0.588 0.217 264) - lighter purple
   - Secondary/Muted/Accent: alpha(white / 4%)

4. **Custom Theme Animation**:
   - `.no-transitions` class suppresses all transitions during theme changes
   - Applied immediately on module load to prevent flash

5. **Component Styling Patterns**:
   - Uses `var(--color-*)` in Tailwind @theme for all color tokens
   - Uses CSS custom properties throughout for theme sync
   - Scrollbar styling for light/dark modes
   - Custom drag region styling for Electron
   - Chat markdown rendering styles
   - Diff panel theme bridges to app palette

---

## 4. React Context & Provider Patterns

### Provider Hierarchy:

```
React.StrictMode
  └── RouterProvider (TanStack Router)
      └── QueryClientProvider (TanStack Query)
          └── StoreProvider (Zustand store)
              └── ToastProvider (Toast UI)
                  └── AnchoredToastProvider (Anchored toast UI)
                      └── [DiffWorkerPoolProvider] (conditionally wrapped)
                          └── <Outlet />
```

### Key Providers:

1. **StoreProvider** (`apps/web/src/store.ts`):
   - Wraps Zustand store
   - Persists app state to localStorage on every change
   - Manages projects, threads, UI state
   - Uses `useStore()` hook throughout app

2. **DiffWorkerPoolProvider** (`apps/web/src/components/DiffWorkerPoolProvider.tsx`):
   - Wraps `@pierre/diffs/react` WorkerPoolContextProvider
   - Theme-aware - syncs diff rendering theme with app theme

7	/** Color tokens that every T3 Code theme must provide. */
8	export interface ThemeColorTokens {
9	  /** App-wide background */
10	  background: string;

12	  foreground: string;

28	  statusBar: string;

36	  id: string;

106	/** Map of theme IDs to definitions for O(1) lookup. */
107	export const BUILT_IN_THEME_MAP: ReadonlyMap<string, ThemeDefinition> = new Map(
108	  BUILT_IN_THEMES.map((t) => [t.id, t]),

111	/** Default theme ID used when nothing is persisted. */
112	export const DEFAULT_THEME_ID: BuiltInThemeId = "t3-dark";
113	
114	/** Resolve a theme definition by ID, falling back to the default theme. */
115	export function resolveThemeById(id: string): ThemeDefinition {
116	  return BUILT_IN_THEME_MAP.get(id) ?? BUILT_IN_THEME_MAP.get(DEFAULT_THEME_ID)!;
117	}

Everything looks clean and correct. Here's a summary of all changes made:

---

## Summary

### New files created:

1. **`packages/shared/src/theme.ts`** — Schema-only module defining the theme type system:
   - `ThemeColorTokens` interface with 11 color tokens: `background`, `foreground`, `accent`, `sidebar`/`sidebarForeground`, `editor`/`editorForeground`, `terminal`/`terminalForeground`, `statusBar`/`statusBarForeground`
   - `ThemeDefinition` interface with `id`, `name`, `appearance` ("light"/"dark"), and `colors`
   - `BuiltInThemeId` type union: `"t3-light" | "t3-dark" | "t3-midnight"`
   - 3 built-in themes: **T3 Light** (clean white/purple), **T3 Dark** (neutral dark/violet), **T3 Midnight** (deep navy/indigo)
   - `BUILT_IN_THEME_MAP` for O(1) lookup + `resolveThemeById()` helper

2. **`apps/web/src/components/ThemeProvider.tsx`** — React context provider:
   - Module-level `useSyncExternalStore` singleton for FOUC prevention (applies theme before React mounts)
   - Persists selection to `localStorage` under `t3code:theme`
   - Applies 11 CSS custom properties (`--theme-*`) on `:root` plus the `.dark` class
   - Cross-tab sync via `StorageEvent`
   - Memoized context value to avoid unnecessary re-renders

3. **`apps/web/src/components/ThemeSelector.tsx`** — Theme picker UI:
   - `ThemePreviewSwatch` — mini IDE mockup showing sidebar, editor, terminal, and status bar using actual theme colors
   - `ThemeOption` — radio button row with swatch, name, appearance label, and "Active" badge
   - `ThemeSelector` — radiogroup of all built-in themes

### Modified files:

4. **`packages/shared/package.json`** — Added `"./theme"` subpath export pointing to `src/theme.ts`

5. **`apps/web/src/hooks/useTheme.ts`** — Rewritten to consume `ThemeContext` from `ThemeProvider`. Same import path, same `resolvedTheme` shape ("light"/"dark") so all existing consumers (`DiffWorkerPoolProvider`, `ChatView`, `DiffPanel`, `ChatMarkdown`) work without changes.

6. **`apps/web/src/index.css`** — Added 11 `--color-theme-*` tokens to the `@theme inline` block so they're usable as Tailwind utilities (e.g. `bg-theme-sidebar`).

7. **`apps/web/src/routes/__root.tsx`** — Wrapped both render paths (loading + connected) with `<ThemeProvider>`.

8. **`apps/web/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx`** — Replaced the old 3-option (system/light/dark) radio group with the new `<ThemeSelector>` component.

### Verification:
- **TypeCheck**: `bun typecheck` passes (web app)
- **Lint**: `bun lint` passes (0 errors, only 2 pre-existing warnings in server code)
- **Tests**: All 315 tests pass
